# Work Packages: Settings + Onboarding

**Inputs**: Design documents from `kitty-specs/010-settings-and-onboarding/`
**Prerequisites**: plan.md (required), spec.md (user stories), research.md, data-model.md, quickstart.md

**Tests**: Not explicitly requested. Manual verification only.

**Organization**: Fine-grained subtasks (`Txxx`) roll up into work packages (`WPxx`). Each work package is independently deliverable and testable.

**Prompt Files**: Each work package references a matching prompt file in `tasks/` generated by `/spec-kitty.tasks`.

## Subtask Format: `[Txxx] [P?] Description`
- **[P]** indicates the subtask can proceed in parallel (different files/components).
- Precise file paths included for each subtask.

---

## Work Package WP01: Foundation — Data Layer + SettingsService (Priority: P0)

**Goal**: Create the E1RMFormula enum, extend HealthProfile with `defaultRestTimeSeconds`, build SettingsService with rebuild orchestration, and wire it into ServiceContainer.
**Independent Test**: SettingsService can fetch/update HealthProfile fields and trigger rebuildAll() on PRService/StatsService.
**Prompt**: `tasks/WP01-foundation-data-and-service.md`
**Estimated Size**: ~350 lines

### Included Subtasks
- [x] T001 Add `defaultRestTimeSeconds: Int?` to HealthProfile model and init
- [x] T002 Create E1RMFormula enum in `Reppo/Data/Enums/E1RMFormula.swift`
- [x] T003 Create SettingsServiceProtocol in `Reppo/Core/Services/Protocols/SettingsServiceProtocol.swift`
- [x] T004 Create SettingsService implementation in `Reppo/Core/Services/SettingsService.swift`
- [x] T005 Wire SettingsService into ServiceContainer

### Implementation Notes
- HealthProfile already exists with `unitPreference`, `includeWarmupsInVolume`, `includeWarmupsInPRs`, `e1RMFormula`. Only add the new optional field `defaultRestTimeSeconds: Int?` with `nil` default.
- SwiftData lightweight migration handles the additive optional field — no manual migration needed.
- E1RMFormula enum: 3 cases (epley, brzycki, lombardi) with `displayName`, `description`, and `calculate(weight:reps:)` methods.
- SettingsService is an `actor` following the existing service pattern. Dependencies: HealthProfileRepository, PRService, StatsService.
- ServiceContainer wiring: SettingsService goes after PRService and StatsService (it depends on both).

### Parallel Opportunities
- T001 (HealthProfile mod) and T002 (E1RMFormula enum) are independent files.
- T003 and T004 are sequential (protocol before implementation).

### Dependencies
- None (starting package).

### Risks & Mitigations
- E1RMFormula enum must use `rawValue: String` matching existing `HealthProfile.e1RMFormula` stored values ("epley", "brzycki", "lombardi").
- SettingsService must NOT create circular dependencies — it consumes PRService and StatsService, neither depends on it.

---

## Work Package WP02: Settings Screen — Main View + ViewModel + Pickers (Priority: P1)

**Goal**: Build the main SettingsView with all 5 sections (GENERAL, WORKOUT PREFERENCES, DATA, BODY, ABOUT), SettingsViewModel, and the 3 picker sheet components.
**Independent Test**: Open Settings tab, see all 5 sections rendered, change unit preference, change e1RM formula, toggle warmup settings with rebuild confirmation, see "Coming Soon" for CSV, see version and Send Feedback in About.
**Prompt**: `tasks/WP02-settings-screen.md`
**Estimated Size**: ~450 lines

### Included Subtasks
- [x] T006 Create SettingsViewModel in `Reppo/Features/Settings/ViewModels/SettingsViewModel.swift`
- [x] T007 Create SettingsView in `Reppo/Features/Settings/Views/SettingsView.swift`
- [x] T008 [P] Create UnitPickerSheet in `Reppo/Features/Settings/Views/Components/UnitPickerSheet.swift`
- [x] T009 [P] Create FormulaPickerSheet in `Reppo/Features/Settings/Views/Components/FormulaPickerSheet.swift`
- [x] T010 [P] Create RestTimePickerSheet in `Reppo/Features/Settings/Views/Components/RestTimePickerSheet.swift`
- [x] T011 Add warmup toggle rebuild confirmation dialogs to SettingsView
- [x] T012 Wire About section — version display + Send Feedback mailto

### Implementation Notes
- SettingsViewModel is `@Observable`, fetches HealthProfile via SettingsService on `.task {}`.
- SettingsView uses `Form` with `Section("HEADER")` for each group. Use `.scrollContentBackground(.hidden)` and `.background(Color.bg)`.
- Units and e1RM Formula rows open as `.sheet`. Default Rest Time opens as `.sheet`.
- WORKOUT PREFERENCES: 2 toggles (warmups in volume, warmups in PRs) + rest time picker. Toggles show confirmation alert before triggering rebuild.
- DATA section: Import/Export buttons show "Coming Soon" alert. Rebuild Stats is a NavigationLink (target built in WP03).
- BODY section: Bodyweight Log is a NavigationLink (target built in WP03).
- ABOUT section: Version from Bundle.main, Send Feedback via mailto: URL.
- All picker sheets follow the same pattern: list of options, current selection highlighted, tap to select + dismiss.

### Parallel Opportunities
- T008, T009, T010 (picker sheets) are independent files — can be built concurrently.
- T006 (ViewModel) must precede T007 (View).

### Dependencies
- Depends on WP01 (SettingsService, E1RMFormula enum).

### Risks & Mitigations
- Warmup toggle confirmation: Must show alert BEFORE saving the change. If user cancels, revert the toggle state.
- Form styling must use dark mode design tokens consistently.

---

## Work Package WP03: Sub-screens — Rebuild Stats + Bodyweight Log (Priority: P1)

**Goal**: Build the RebuildStatsView (3 rebuild buttons with confirmation/progress) and the BodyweightLogView (trend chart + entry list + add entry).
**Independent Test**: Navigate from Settings to Rebuild Stats, tap Rebuild All with confirmation, see progress overlay. Navigate to Bodyweight Log, see trend chart, add an entry, verify it appears.
**Prompt**: `tasks/WP03-rebuild-stats-and-bodyweight-log.md`
**Estimated Size**: ~450 lines

### Included Subtasks
- [x] T013 Create RebuildStatsView in `Reppo/Features/Settings/Views/RebuildStatsView.swift`
- [x] T014 Create BodyweightLogViewModel in `Reppo/Features/Settings/ViewModels/BodyweightLogViewModel.swift`
- [x] T015 Create BodyweightLogView in `Reppo/Features/Settings/Views/BodyweightLogView.swift`
- [x] T016 Implement bodyweight trend chart (Swift Charts LineMark + PointMark)
- [x] T017 Create Add Bodyweight Entry sheet (weight input + date picker)
- [x] T018 Implement entry deletion (swipe-to-delete on entry list)

### Implementation Notes
- **RebuildStatsView**: Explanation text, 3 buttons ([Rebuild PRs], [Rebuild Stats], [Rebuild All]). Confirmation alert before each. Modal ProgressView overlay during rebuild. Completion alert on success. Error alert on failure.
- **BodyweightLogViewModel**: `@Observable`, uses BodyweightService for CRUD, SettingsService for unit preference. Entries sorted by date DESC.
- **BodyweightLogView**: NavigationStack. Top: trend chart (~200pt height). Below: chronological entry list with swipe-to-delete. Toolbar [+Add] button.
- **Trend chart**: Swift Charts `LineMark` + `PointMark`. Y-axis auto-scaled (no zero). Unit-aware (convert kg → lbs if imperial). Date-based X-axis.
- **Add Entry sheet**: Weight text field + date picker. Validates non-zero weight. Saves via BodyweightService.saveEntry().
- **Empty state**: When no entries exist, show message encouraging first entry + prominent Add button.
- RebuildStatsView can reuse SettingsViewModel or have its own lightweight state — use SettingsService directly.

### Parallel Opportunities
- T013 (RebuildStatsView) and T014-T018 (Bodyweight Log) are largely independent concerns.

### Dependencies
- Depends on WP02 (SettingsView provides NavigationLinks to these sub-screens, SettingsViewModel patterns).

### Risks & Mitigations
- Rebuild should block UI interaction (modal overlay) to prevent data inconsistency.
- Chart performance: ~365 entries max — well within Swift Charts limits.
- Unit conversion in chart: must apply at display time, not to stored data.

---

## Work Package WP04: Onboarding Flow (Priority: P1)

**Goal**: Build the 5-screen onboarding flow shown on first launch: Welcome, Units, e1RM Formula, Bodyweight (optional), Import prompt (stub).
**Independent Test**: Delete @AppStorage("hasCompletedOnboarding"), launch app, see 5-step onboarding, complete it, arrive at Calendar tab. Re-launch — onboarding does NOT show.
**Prompt**: `tasks/WP04-onboarding-flow.md`
**Estimated Size**: ~450 lines

### Included Subtasks
- [ ] T019 Create OnboardingStep enum in `Reppo/Features/Onboarding/OnboardingStep.swift`
- [ ] T020 Create OnboardingViewModel in `Reppo/Features/Onboarding/ViewModels/OnboardingViewModel.swift`
- [ ] T021 Create OnboardingContainerView in `Reppo/Features/Onboarding/Views/OnboardingContainerView.swift`
- [ ] T022 [P] Create WelcomeStepView in `Reppo/Features/Onboarding/Views/WelcomeStepView.swift`
- [ ] T023 [P] Create UnitsStepView in `Reppo/Features/Onboarding/Views/UnitsStepView.swift`
- [ ] T024 [P] Create FormulaStepView in `Reppo/Features/Onboarding/Views/FormulaStepView.swift`
- [ ] T025 [P] Create BodyweightStepView in `Reppo/Features/Onboarding/Views/BodyweightStepView.swift`
- [ ] T026 [P] Create ImportStepView in `Reppo/Features/Onboarding/Views/ImportStepView.swift`

### Implementation Notes
- **OnboardingStep enum**: View-layer only, `Int` raw value (0-4). Properties: `isSkippable`, `skipBehavior`.
- **OnboardingViewModel**: `@Observable`. Tracks currentStep, selectedUnit, selectedFormula, bodyweightInput. `next()`, `skip()`, `finish()` methods. On `finish()`: saves settings via SettingsService, saves bodyweight via BodyweightService if entered.
- **OnboardingContainerView**: `TabView` with `.tabViewStyle(.page(indexDisplayMode: .never))`. Manual Next/Skip/Finish buttons per step. Page indicator dots.
- **WelcomeStepView**: App icon/name, tagline, single "Get Started" button.
- **UnitsStepView**: Metric/Imperial selection with visual feedback. Default: metric.
- **FormulaStepView**: 3 formula options (Epley, Brzycki, Lombardi) with descriptions. Default: Epley.
- **BodyweightStepView**: Optional weight entry. Skip button prominent. "Enter your bodyweight for more accurate tracking" messaging.
- **ImportStepView**: "Migrating from another app?" with stub action ("Coming in a future update"). Skip/Finish button.
- All steps skippable except Welcome. Sensible defaults applied on skip.

### Parallel Opportunities
- T022-T025 (individual step views) are independent files — highly parallelizable.
- T019 and T020 must precede T021 (container references both).

### Dependencies
- Depends on WP01 (SettingsService for saving preferences, E1RMFormula enum for formula selection).

### Risks & Mitigations
- Onboarding must NOT gate app usage — skipping is always allowed.
- `@AppStorage("hasCompletedOnboarding")` must be set to `true` at the END of `finish()`, not at the start — prevents partial completion from hiding onboarding.
- TabView page style must disable swipe gestures — use buttons only for navigation to maintain control.

---

## Work Package WP05: Integration + App Wiring (Priority: P1)

**Goal**: Wire onboarding gate into ReppoApp, replace SettingsPlaceholderView with real SettingsView in ContentView, connect all dependencies, update project.pbxproj, verify build.
**Independent Test**: App launches. First launch shows onboarding → completes → Calendar tab. Settings tab shows full settings screen. Build succeeds with 0 errors.
**Prompt**: `tasks/WP05-integration-and-wiring.md`
**Estimated Size**: ~300 lines

### Included Subtasks
- [x] T027 Add @AppStorage("hasCompletedOnboarding") conditional in `Reppo/App/ReppoApp.swift`
- [x] T028 Replace SettingsPlaceholderView with SettingsView in `Reppo/App/ContentView.swift`
- [x] T029 Wire dependencies — pass services to Settings and Onboarding views via init/environment
- [x] T030 Add all new file references to `Reppo.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj`
- [x] T031 Build verification — compile project, verify 0 errors

### Implementation Notes
- **ReppoApp.swift**: Add `@AppStorage("hasCompletedOnboarding") private var hasCompletedOnboarding = false`. Use `if/else` in body to show `OnboardingContainerView` or `ContentView`. Pass `onComplete` closure that sets the flag to `true`.
- **ContentView.swift**: Find `SettingsPlaceholderView()` reference (line ~64) and replace with `SettingsView(viewModel:)`. Pass SettingsViewModel initialized with SettingsService from ServiceContainer.
- **Dependency wiring**: SettingsView needs SettingsService. BodyweightLogView needs BodyweightService + SettingsService. OnboardingContainerView needs SettingsService + BodyweightService.
- **project.pbxproj**: Add all ~18 new Swift files. Follow existing patterns for file references and build phases.
- **Build verification**: `xcodebuild build` or open in Xcode and verify 0 errors.

### Parallel Opportunities
- T027 (ReppoApp) and T028 (ContentView) modify different files.
- T030 (pbxproj) should be done last after all files exist.

### Dependencies
- Depends on WP02, WP03, WP04 (all views and ViewModels must exist to wire them).

### Risks & Mitigations
- pbxproj merge conflicts: This file is notoriously hard to merge. Keep changes minimal and precise.
- Missing dependency injection: Verify all ViewModels receive their required services at init time.
- `@AppStorage` must use consistent key string ("hasCompletedOnboarding") everywhere.

---

## Dependency & Execution Summary

```
WP01: Foundation (no deps)
 ├── WP02: Settings Screen (depends on WP01)
 │    └── WP03: Rebuild Stats + Bodyweight Log (depends on WP02)
 ├── WP04: Onboarding Flow (depends on WP01)
 └── WP05: Integration (depends on WP02, WP03, WP04)
```

- **Sequence**: WP01 → [WP02 + WP04 in parallel] → WP03 → WP05
- **Parallelization**: WP02 and WP04 can run concurrently after WP01 completes (they share WP01 as a base but work on different Features/ directories).
- **MVP Scope**: WP01 + WP02 + WP05 constitute a minimal Settings release (no bodyweight chart or onboarding). Full MVP: all 5 WPs.
